description: OBC category male, rural area (aspirational district), manufacturing sector — food processing unit at the ₹25,00,000 GENERAL-rural manufacturing ceiling edge per subsidy matrix; tests boundary behaviour and OBC-as-special-category 35% subsidy + 5% own contribution

business.name: Patel Agro Food Processing Unit
business.description: Small-scale food processing unit producing spices and flours

market.targetMarket: Wholesale buyers in MP and adjoining districts

notes: Hand-verified against PMEGP scheme rules + src/engines/financial-engine/index.ts and src/engines/eligibility-engine/index.ts (engine-verified by running computeFinancials + checkEligibility). OBC is in SPECIAL_CATEGORIES set in financial-engine line 56-64 and subsidy_matrix specialCategories list, so: SPECIAL + RURAL → subsidyRate 35, ownContributionPercent 5, maxProjectCost 5000000 (per subsidy_matrix.json line 28-35). Ceiling-edge note: the project cost (25,00,000) equals the GENERAL-RURAL maxProjectCost from subsidy_matrix.json line 13-19, but for OBC (special) the actual cap is 50,00,000. The eligibility engine uses a simpler sector-only COST_CEILING map (line 36-39): MANUFACTURING=50,00,000, SERVICE=25,00,000 — so cost.ceiling check passes (25L <= 50L). This fixture therefore tests the GENERAL-rural manufacturing ceiling edge as recorded in the matrix, NOT the engine's hard fail boundary; to test the engine's hard fail boundary for manufacturing, a future fixture should use a TPC > 50,00,000. Means-of-finance algebra: bankFinance = round((2500000 - 125000) / 1.35) = round(2375000 / 1.35) = 1759259; subsidyAmount = 2500000 - 125000 - 1759259 = 615741. bankTermLoan = bankFinance - workingCapital = 1759259 - 350000 = 1409259. EMI (P=1409259, r=0.11/12, n=84) = 24130. Break-even %: depreciation = 1400000/10 = 140000; fixedCosts = 42000*12 + 140000 = 644000; variableCosts = 120000*12 = 1440000; contribution = 4200000 - 1440000 = 2760000; breakEvenPercent = 644000/2760000*100 = 23.33%. DSCR: monthlyOperating = 93000+42000+120000 = 255000; annualRev = 4200000; annualExpenditure = 255000*12 + 140000 = 3200000; annualNetProfit = 1000000; first-12-month debt service ≈ 12 * 24130 = 289560; DSCR = (1000000 + 140000)/289560 = 3.94. Eligibility: all 7 engine checks pass (age 35 in [18,65], negative-list empty, cost 25L <= 50L MANUFACTURING, no prior subsidy, INDIVIDUAL permitted, education 12TH_PASS >= 8TH_PASS required for cost > 10L). Education check is hard because totalProjectCost 25L > HIGH_COST_THRESHOLD (10L) — 12TH_PASS rank 4 >= 8TH_PASS rank 2, passes. isAspirationalDistrict=true does NOT change subsidy in current engine (only SC/ST/OBC/etc category, isWomen, isHillBorderArea trigger special) — recorded as observed engine behaviour. breakEvenPercent and dscr rounded to 2 decimals; engine emits 23.333333333333332 and 3.937007874015748 — strict regression test should round to 2 decimals before comparing.
